body:
Thanks for finding this ambiguous method @giordano!

This is a breaking change as we won't be able to do `push!(simulation.output_writers, output_writer)` anymore. This does not seem like a common pattern though.

It's an ordered dict and I think users should be forced to pick a key for each output writer and diagnostic they add. But let me know if anyone else feels otherwise!

labels.description: Concerning a change which breaks the API
labels.name: breaking change :broken_heart:

title: (v0.104.0) Remove support for pushing output writers and diagnostics into ordered dicts without a key

body:
This PR implements NetCDF supoprt for `FieldTimeSeries`. In the process it also reorganizes the code in `OceananigansNCDasasetsExt` a bit (but not the logic; I just broke it apart into different files).

Closes https://github.com/CliMA/Oceananigans.jl/issues/3935

To-do list (not all of this for this PR but I'll do what I can):
- [x] Make `FieldTimeSeries` work with `InMemory()` backend
- [ ] Make `OnDisk()` backend work
- [ ] Make `FieldDataset` work
- [ ] Make chunked `FieldTimeSeries` work
- [ ] Allow reading `FieldBoundaryCondition`s from `NetCDF` output
- [ ] Allow for NetCDF output `with_halos` and non-default `indices` (why isn't that allowed?)
- [x] Test output readers with `NetCDFWriter`

Necessary NetCDFWriter enhancements:

- [x] Write `Field` location to NetCDF files along `Field`s
- [x] Write `Field` indices to NetCDF
- [ ] ~Write boundary conditions to NetCDF somehow~
title: Make `FieldTimeSeries` work with NetCDF output

body: This PR adds developer docs for implementing a turbulence closure, using Pacanowski-Philander as an example.
title: Implement developer docs for TurbulenceClosures
Reduce CFL in ocean mixing and convection example

body: In Julia v1.11+, the [`[sources]` section](https://pkgdocs.julialang.org/v1/toml-files/#The-%5Bsources%5D-section) of `Project.toml` is a much saner way to refer to local packages than manually fiddling with `Pkg.develop` every single time.
title: Add `Oceananigans` to the `docs` environment with `[sources]` section
